projects.title: INDEXO
projects.teammates: Reinis Vitols, Alberts Pumpurs, Martins Tuls, Anna Lipenberga

blocks.subtitle: WHAT IS INDEXO?
blocks.description: INDEXO is a modern financial company in Latvia focused on transparent investing, pension management, and digital banking.

blocks.subtitle: WHAT WAS MY ROLE?
blocks.description: The bank officially launched on 28 August 2024, and I joined the team less than a month later as a UI/UX designer. Over the year, I worked on both the app and website, improving key user flows.

sections.title: SOLUTION
sections.subtitle: LOAN PRODUCT DISPLAY
description: When I joined INDEXO, the app offered only one loan option — a consumer loan.
As the product grew, the team introduced mortgage refinancing and later home equity, so we had to redesign the structure to clearly show the differences between products.

sections.title: BIGGEST PROJECTS I TOOK A PART IN

cards.title: Loan products
cards.text: I helped reorganize the loan section so users could compare and understand multiple loan types more clearly.

cards.title: VISA+
cards.text: I designed the full flow for using phone numbers for global money transfers while reducing confusion with existing local transfer features.

cards.title: Other projects
cards.text: I also worked on the Junior Account feature, retirement planning tool, website pages, testing, and automation-related improvements.

projects.title: EtherLocked
projects.teammates: Rebeka Forgach, Eliza Drazniece, Mate Forgach

blocks.subtitle: WHAT IS ETHERLOCKED?
blocks.description: The game explores the fragile state between dreaming and waking through a surreal first-person puzzle experience. The story follows Orion, a 17-year-old separated from his body during sleep paralysis, with storytelling delivered entirely through environmental interaction.

blocks.subtitle: WHAT WAS MY ROLE?
blocks.description: EtherLocked was a year-long university project and our first major game development experience as a team. The assignment was to create a first-person puzzle game without NPCs or inventory systems. I worked as the developer, UX designer, and UI designer, building interactions and shaping the overall player experience.

sections.title: IDEA & STORY
sections.subtitle: A WORLD BETWEEN DREAMING AND WAKING
description: The game explores the fragile state between dreaming and waking through a surreal first-person puzzle experience.
The story follows Orion, a 17-year-old separated from his body during sleep paralysis, with storytelling delivered entirely through environmental interaction.

sections.title: GAMEPLAY & MECHANICS
sections.subtitle: INTERACTION-BASED PUZZLE DESIGN
description: The core mechanics revolve around object interaction: picking up, rotating, examining, and throwing items.
Special interactions like the clock and padlock use dedicated inspection views, while drawers, doors, diary pages, and the mirror each have their own systems.

cards.title: Long planning phase
cards.text: A large amount of time was spent planning the story, puzzle flow, and structure before development could begin.

cards.title: Puzzle logic
cards.text: Connecting the diary, tickets, clock, and padlock systems took a lot of experimentation and redesign.

cards.title: UI state management
cards.text: Handling menus, interactions, and camera transitions without conflicts was one of the most technical parts of the project.

blocks.subtitle: WHAT IS THE PROJECT?
blocks.description: This was a half-year project where we created a mystical French deck that also visualizes the rules of Shithead.

blocks.subtitle: WHAT WAS MY ROLE?
blocks.description: I worked as a designer and illustrator, helping build the deck’s visual identity and creature designs.

sections.title: VISUAL SYSTEM
sections.subtitle: FOUR ELEMENTS
description: The deck follows a dark four-element theme: water, air, earth, and fire.
Special rule cards such as 2, 3, 7, and 10 were given unique creatures to visually explain their role in gameplay.

cards.title: Earth
cards.text: One suit was designed around an earthy mystical theme with dark creature illustrations.

cards.title: Air
cards.text: The air suit focused on lightness and contrast while staying consistent with the overall style.

cards.title: Fire & Water
cards.text: The remaining suits completed the elemental system and helped the full deck feel cohesive and thematic.

blocks.subtitle: WHAT IS COURT CHEESTER?
blocks.description: Court Cheester is an arcade hopper game made during a weekend game jam with the theme “Make me laugh.”

blocks.subtitle: WHAT WAS MY ROLE?
blocks.description: I worked as the developer and also contributed to UX and UI design while my teammates created the art.

sections.title: GAME JAM RESULT
sections.subtitle: FAST, FUN, PLAYABLE
description: Despite the short timeframe, our team created a complete and playful arcade hopper experience.
The project focused on speed, humor, and a fun, readable gameplay loop.

blocks.subtitle: WHAT IS THE PROJECT?
blocks.description: This project was created as my bachelor’s thesis and focuses on developing a real-time system that uses computer vision to evaluate how correctly a person performs a sit-up.

blocks.subtitle: WHAT WAS MY ROLE?
blocks.description: I handled the research, model comparison, implementation, testing, and evaluation of the system.

sections.title: THEORY
sections.subtitle: MODEL AND DATASET RESEARCH
description: I researched multiple pose estimation models and datasets to compare accuracy, visualization approaches, and speed.
This helped determine the most suitable method for real-time exercise feedback.

sections.title: PRACTICAL PART 1
sections.subtitle: RULE-BASED APPROACH
description: The first version detected sit-up quality using keypoint-based rules for upper-body movement and hand position.
Testing revealed challenges with hidden keypoints, side angles, and crossed arms.

cards.title: Theory work
cards.text: Five pose models and multiple public datasets were compared before choosing the best direction.

cards.title: Rule-based system
cards.text: The first version worked but struggled with occlusion, camera angle variation, and more complex poses.

cards.title: Improved model
cards.text: Training on custom datasets significantly improved the accuracy of upper-body and hand-position detection.
